text: default. makes network request returns wording computed context.
code: # Model Fit and Evaluation Report  ## Scope This is a descriptive summary of a multiclass task for `Species`, covering 4 model(s) and 4 feature(s).  ## Did the model improve on a simple baseline? The tuned neural network was evaluated on 36 held-out test rows. Its **log_loss** was 0.0844. That is a 92.3% improvement over the intercept-only baseline (1.0986).  ## What the main metric means **log_loss:** Probability error that penalizes confident wrong answers; lower is better.  ## Can the probabilities be taken literally? The descriptive binned calibration gap was 2.4% across 3 groups. Mean reported probability was 98.3% and observed frequency was 97.2%. This depends on the held-out sample and grouping; it is not a population guarantee. Read it beside log loss and Brier score.  ## Score cautions - **caution:** Only 36 rows were available for held-out evaluation. Treat the scores as preliminary and validate on more representative unseen rows.  ## How automatic tuning selected the model 8 configurations across 3 model families were compared with 5 training-only folds. The selection metric was log_loss. The one-standard-error (prefer the reviewed family priority, then the least-flexible near-best setting within that family) rule selected the neural network with hidden units = 2, weight decay = 0.03. Its resampled log_loss was 0.09475. The resampling-selected configuration was `neural_02`; the actual final fitted configuration was `neural_02` (neural network). A recorded refit fallback was not needed. That resampled score selected a configuration; it is not the final performance estimate. The held-out score above evaluated the selected, refitted model on different rows.  ## What kinds of models were retained? The family descriptions below are prior/model-capacity knowledge. They describe what a family can represent, not patterns proven to have been used by these fitted models. - `main_model`: neural via nnet; can represent smooth and flexible; interaction capacity: automatic through hidden units. - `linear_model`: linear via nnet; can represent none unless encoded in features; interaction capacity: none unless specified in features. - `tree_model`: tree via rpart; can represent stepwise; interaction capacity: automatic along tree paths.  ## What did the retained models do differently? This section is computed evidence from common evaluation rows, not a claim inferred from model-family names. The largest average paired prediction difference was between `tree_model` and `linear_model`: 0.0887 using total-variation distance between class-probability vectors. Descriptive comparison of supplied fitted models on common evaluation rows; not causal inference, uncertainty coverage, or a deployment rule.  ## Computed feature evidence The leading descriptive permutation-importance features are Petal.Length, Petal.Width, Sepal.Width. They are computed evidence of model reliance on the evaluation data, not family-capacity claims, and should not be read as causal effects.  ## What to do next Inspect the held-out errors and explanation evidence, check whether the data represent the intended use, and validate the result on new data before relying on it.  ## Required limitations Permutation repeats quantify computation-level variability, not population uncertainty. Feature explanations do not establish causality, fairness, safety, or deployment readiness. Use held-out data, domain review, and external validation.  Data disclosure: Aggregated diagnostics only. No raw rows, fitted model objects, case-level predictions, or secrets are included.

code: memo <- generate_natural_language_report(   fit,   importance_data = importance,   provider = "gemini",   temperature = 0.2,   max_tokens = 4000,   fallback = FALSE ) # Evaluation of Tuned Neural Network for Species Classification  ## Held-out performance On the held-out test set, the reference tuned neural network achieved a final log_loss of 0.08206, which is a 92.5% relative improvement over the simple baseline score of 1.09861. Log_loss is a metric of probability error that penalizes confident wrong answers, where lower values are better. This final held-out performance is distinct from the training-resampled selection score of 0.09566, which was calculated using 5 training-only folds during the automatic tuning process.  ## Patterns used by the model - The model's predictions are descriptively associated with the top features Petal.Length, Petal.Width, and Sepal.Width. - Probability calibration checks show a binned gap of 0.0232 across 3 groups, where the model's mean reported probability was 98.0% and the observed frequency was 97.2%.  ## Cautions - Only 36 rows were available for the held-out evaluation, meaning the performance metrics may not generalize to broader populations.  ## Practical next steps - Treat the current evaluation scores as preliminary and validate the model on a larger, more representative set of unseen rows. - Verify that future input data maintains a low rate of missingness, as the current check showed 0 model inputs crossing the 5.0% practical rate-change flag.  ## Required interpretation boundaries - This memo summarizes predictive associations in the supplied evaluation; it does not establish causality. - Schema validation checks the response format, not whether generated statements are true. Verify every statement against the computed report. - This analysis is not a fairness, safety, compliance, or deployment certification. Validate it on relevant new data with domain review. Parameter names exact adapter contracts: linear parameters; regularized uses alpha path_fraction; additive uses k, gamma, select; tree uses maxdepth, cp, minsplit; forest uses num.trees, mtry, min.node.size, sample.fraction, splitrule; boosting uses nrounds, eta, max_depth, min_child_weight, subsample, colsample_bytree, reg_alpha, reg_lambda; neural uses size decay; kernel uses cost, gamma_multiplier, epsilon (fixed 0.1 classification backend ignores ); neighbors uses k, distance, kernel; mars uses degree nprune. Family budgets exact positive configuration counts. names must exactly match learner families requested autoxplain(). supplied, leave max_models = NULL; sum budgets replaces automatic portfolio budget. Supplied fold IDs define ordinary V-fold resampling: row validated fitting rows assigned every fold. rolling-origin forward-chaining specification. avoid ambiguity AutoXplainR's automatic holdout split, fold IDs accepted test_data supplied explicitly autoxplain(). Candidate losses one-standard-error uncertainty weighted number validation rows fold; RMSE uses pooled squared loss delta-method standard error RMSE scale.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Added dependency-light autoxplain(data, target) path creates reproducible holdout, fits simple baseline understandable primary model, evaluates task-appropriate metrics definitions. Added local linear, logistic, multinomial logistic workflows regression, binary classification, multiclass classification. H2O remains available explicitly engine = "h2o". Added model_set = "tuned", tuning_results(), ten-family learner registry. recommended portfolio compares linear, regularized, additive, tree, forest, boosting models; extended mode adds neural, radial-kernel, nearest-neighbor, MARS models. Every backend shares one serializable prediction contract tuned fold-specific preprocessing inside outer training set. Added portfolio-aware automatic search budgets, space-filling low-budget grids, stable per-configuration seeds, explicit dependency/version status, retained family winners, paired --fold predictions, deterministic fallback full-data refit fails. Explicit model budgets silently capped. Added optional tuning_control() escape hatch validated custom family grids exact budgets, RMSE/MAE log-loss/Brier selection, ordinary supplied V-fold IDs, --fold retention control, configurable failure handling. Beginner portfolio defaults remain unchanged. one-standard-error rule now uses reviewed family priority followed family-specific flexibility proxy; raw proxy values never compared across unrelated model families. final holdout remains untouched selection full-training refit complete. Added compare_model_behavior() separate reviewed model-capacity cards computed -row prediction disagreement optional permutation- importance evidence across retained families. Added compare_model_effects() base-R plot method aligned, support-aware ALE/PDP comparisons one named feature across retained families. Curves retain prediction target explicitly scoped fitted-function descriptions rather causal effects. Added render_model_report(), standalone beginner-first report leads modeling question, held-baseline comparison, metric definitions revealing feature patterns collapsed evidence audit. Added evaluation-row predictions, regression error diagnostics, classification confusion matrices, Brier scores, held-probability calibration groups, actionable warnings sample-size baseline-performance failure modes. Added calibration_diagnostics() adjacent report explanation compares grouped probabilities observed frequencies making sample- grouping-dependence explicit. Added threshold_diagnostics() default binary-report view showing sensitivity, specificity, precision, accuracy, false positives, false negatives change 0.5 decision convention moves. report never presents cutoff optimized held-rows validated. Added opt-subgroup_performance() subgroup = report support comparing held-metrics across one explicitly chosen column. Small groups remain visible flagged, output rejects fairness-certification language. Added missingness_shift() using pre-imputation, per-predictor rates retained preprocessing provenance. Guided results flag practical training-versus- evaluation differences without presenting statistical test general drift check. guided workflow now rejects unsupported non-finite inputs, removes records constant predictors, reapplies fitted preprocessing recipe raw evaluation data supplied later. Added model_set = "comparison", dependency-light candidate set pre-specified statistical model, two decision-tree complexities, simple baseline. Held-candidate ranks remain descriptive rather silently replacing primary model. Added candidate-set-relative Pareto analysis model_tradeoffs() rebuilt plot_model_comparison() around explicit performance-versus-size trade-offs rather subjective composite score. Guided reports now include plain-language multi-model trade-section dependency-free Pareto SVG whenever two candidates fitted. Added prediction_ambiguity() comparison-report section finds held-rows supplied non-baseline candidates predict different values, classes, probability distributions. Candidate performance remains beside disagreement weak-model variation mislabeled uncertainty. Numerical fit warnings captured model diagnostics translated actionable report warning instead lost printed without context. Repositioned model-agnostic explanation evidence audit advanced reliability layer behind beginner workflow. Added explain_model() validated prediction contract regression, binary classification, multiclass classification. Explicit tasks now reject incompatible outcomes; multi-column probabilities must identify class semantics rather relying column position. Added audit_explanations() combine repeated-importance stability, feature dependence, near-optimal-model importance ranges, prediction agreement, prioritized findings, explicit permitted-claim labels. Added render_explanation_report(), standalone accessible HTML artifact runtime dependency H2O, Plotly, Flexdashboard, LLM.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Rebuilt permutation importance retain repeat-level values, sign stability, grouped-feature permutations, optional blocked permutations, Monte Carlo intervals whose interpretation stated explicitly. Added accumulated local effects (ALE) default feature-effect method. Rebuilt PDP estimation relative support predictor-dependence diagnostics. Added supplied-model Rashomon diagnostics: empirical near-optimal membership, feature-importance ranges, rank agreement, prediction ambiguity. Numeric two-level outcomes now detected binary classification instead silently becoming regression targets.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Replaced hard-coded three-column tuning dispatcher learner-family registry generic list-column search plan. learner_catalog() now exposes distinction model family, backend, supported tasks, reviewed plain-language behavior card. Tuning records requested fold-effective hyperparameters. Seeds derived effective configuration, adding reordering learner silently change another initialization configurations collapsed fold-specific bounds truly fit identically. Supplying new rows as_explainers() now always reapplies fitted outcome-level schema contract, even predictor preprocessing disabled; reversed factor levels silently invert binary probability semantics. Balanced accuracy macro recall now unavailable evaluation rows omit trained class, rather averaging classes happen present. Held-fold-validation rows never moved training repair categorical levels. Training recipes instead learn modal fallback novel predictor levels, record mapping counts, retain strict novel_level_strategy = "error" option. H2O AutoML now optional fitting adapter; core audits use lightweight runtime dependency set. H2O adapter now creates automatic outer holdout preprocessing, learns recipe training rows , never reuses holdout AutoML validation frame. nfolds = 0 rejected unless explicit validation data set supplied, preventing ranking training error alone. H2O’s engine leaderboard now retained separately engine-neutral outer-evaluation leaderboard. model selected H2O cross-validation, explicitly supplied validation frame nfolds = 0, remains primary; common-row ranks descriptive include intercept-baseline. Explicit evaluation data remain outside H2O validation default. Exact duplicate-valued records across supplied training evaluation data trigger configurable possible-leakage diagnostic rather treated proof observational units overlap. H2O provenance now distinguishes time-limited best-effort seeded searches reproducible fixed-model-budget mode records Deep Learning caveat. Live H2O smoke tests now cover regression, binary, multiclass contracts. also guard probability-column normalization keeps hard labels coercing valid multiclass probabilities character data. Preprocessing conservative, returns reusable recipe, applies fitted levels/imputation values evaluation data. Identifier removal guessed ordinal conversion opt-. Added engine-neutral preprocess_data() name; preprocess_for_h2o() retained compatibility alias. Replaced generated 2,300-line Flexdashboard implementation evidence-report pipeline used model-agnostic callers. Replaced fixed, ranking-first LLM prompts local deterministic reports explicit aggregated-data-Gemini, Groq, Cloudflare Workers AI, Ollama, OpenRouter, custom OpenAI-compatible adapters. Local deterministic reporting always default, even API key present, provider/model/fallback details attached provenance. Added dated provider decision guide covering current free access, privacy, reproducibility, setup, structured-output capabilities. Groq default now uses schema-capable openai/gpt-oss-120b. Schema-capable narrative providers now return validated five-field object AutoXplainR renders locally fixed causal, fairness, safety, external-validation boundaries. Invalid JSON falls back transparently structured-output use retained provenance. Narrative list fields total length bounded, non-loopback custom endpoints must use HTTPS credentials sent plaintext networks. Migrated Gemini current Interactions API store = false provider-recommended default temperature. OpenRouter schema requests require compatible routed provider; unsupported adapters remain explicitly unstructured. Added complete, reproducible local live Gemini output snapshots. live Gemini check exposed reasoning-token exhaustion previous response budget, default now 4,000 tokens incomplete interactions return targeted recovery guidance. Removed bundled third-party datasets whose provenance redistribution terms documented.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Added CRAN-oriented metadata, automated checks, coverage, pkgdown deployment, issue forms, contribution security policies, citation metadata, release checklist. Enforced 80% statement-coverage floor ordinary package suite; Plotly/native-engine live H2O paths run separate integration jobs. Added optional-engine matrix covering every advertised supported task glmnet, mgcv, ranger, XGBoost, e1071, kknn, earth. Every case exercises fitting, permutation importance, ALE, explanation audit, aligned cross-model effects. Split fast model-agnostic unit tests opt-H2O integration tests made version-tag releases wait native, coverage, lint, spelling, H2O, source-package, permanent-link gates. Responsive browser QA now guards long diagnostic codes widening standalone report beyond mobile viewport retaining table-local horizontal scrolling. Rebuilt examples vignette around held-evaluation explicit interpretation boundaries.
